name: s5in-c-bva-test-generator
description: Automatically generates Google Test test cases for C functions using boundary value analysis — by Maksym Redchenko. Right-click a function → get tests.

cTestGenerator.testDensity.markdownDescription:
Controls how many boundary test cases are generated per function.

- `minimal` — min, max, zero only (fewest tests)
- `standard` — full BVA: min, min+1, max−1, max (**default**)
- `exhaustive` — all boundary classes including near-zero, infinity, overflow

cTestGenerator.numberOfRandomValues.markdownDescription: Number of additional random test values generated per parameter. These are appended after the boundary-value tests. Set to `0` to disable random test generation.

cTestGenerator.enableBoundaryNaN.markdownDescription: Include `NAN` in the boundary values generated for floating-point parameters (`float`, `double`). Disable to skip NaN test cases.

cTestGenerator.enableBoundaryInfinity.markdownDescription: Include `INFINITY` and `-INFINITY` in the boundary values generated for floating-point parameters (`float`, `double`). Disable to skip infinity test cases.

cTestGenerator.enableBoundaryZero.markdownDescription: Include zero and near-zero boundary values (e.g. `0`, `0.0f`, `-0.0f`, `FLT_EPSILON`) in test generation. Disable to skip zero-boundary test cases.

cTestGenerator.outputFormat.markdownDescription:
Output format for generated test files.

- `googletest` — Google Test `TEST(…)` macros (**default**)
- `plain` — plain C function stubs without a test framework

cTestGenerator.includeNegativeTests.markdownDescription: Generate negative/invalid-input test cases (e.g. `NULL` pointers, negative sizes). Disable to produce only valid-input boundary tests.

cTestGenerator.testFileNamingPattern.markdownDescription: Pattern for the generated test file name. `{filename}` is replaced with the source file's base name (without extension). For example, with the default `test_{filename}`, opening `math.c` produces `test_math.cpp`.

scripts.compile: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js
scripts.package: npm run check-types && npm run lint && node esbuild.js --production
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile && npm run lint
